topic.name: Major League Baseball History
topic.description: The documents collectively discuss the careers and contributions of various players in Major League Baseball, highlighting their achievements, teams played for, and impacts on the sport.

1: Stan Benjamin was a right fielder in Major League Baseball for five seasons.
He played in minor league baseball for ten seasons.
2: Roger Peckinpaugh was an American professional baseball player.
He played in Major League Baseball from 1910 through 1927.
3: Justin De Fratus is an American professional baseball relief pitcher in the Washington Nationals organization.
He was selected in the 11th round of the 2007 Major League Baseball draft.

It originated in the 1930s as the signature song of one of the Glasgow razor gangs.
Document 9: Sir Bevil Grenville's Monument commemorates the heroism of the Civil War Royalist commander Sir Bevil Grenville.
It was erected in 1720 to honor Grenville's legacy after he fell mortally wounded at the Battle of Lansdowne.

topic.name: Military History of Australia and Germany
topic.description: This topic encompasses the military history and contributions of Australian and German forces during World War I and World War II.

1: Friedrich Foertsch was a German general serving during World War II.
Foertsch was awarded the Knight's Cross of the Iron Cross for his leadership in the defensive battles at the Leningrad Front.
2: The 27th Battalion was an infantry battalion of the Australian Army raised for service during World War I.
During World War II the battalion was used mainly in a garrison role until the last year of the war.
3: The 2/10th Battalion was raised for overseas service as part of the Second Australian Imperial Force during World War II.
The battalion's first experience of combat came during an attack against the Italian garrison at Giarabub in March 1941.
4: Central Flying School was re-established to train flying instructors for the Empire Air Training Scheme during World War II.
By the end of World War II, the school had produced more than 3,600 instructors.
5: No. 5 Airfield Construction Squadron served in the New Guinea Campaign and Borneo Campaign during World War II.
The squadron was one of only a few RAAF airfield construction units to be retained at the end of the war.
6: Western Area Command was primarily responsible for air defence and aerial reconnaissance during World War II.
Its aircraft conducted anti-submarine operations throughout the war.
7: Major General Sir John Evelyn Duigan served with the New Zealand Military Forces during the First and Second World Wars.
He was Chief of the General Staff of the New Zealand Military Forces from 1937 to 1941.
8: Les Clisby was an Australian fighter ace of World War II, credited with sixteen aerial victories before being killed in action.
He became known as a highly aggressive fighter pilot during the Battle of France.
9: The 174th Infantry Brigade has been a reserve unit of the United States Army, deployed for both World War I and World War II.
It provides operational training readiness for units in the First Army.
10: No. 30 Transport Unit operated during the Korean War, supporting RAAF fighter squadrons.
The unit transported supplies and equipment to Australian and Commonwealth ground forces.

Document 2: Jocelin of Wells was a medieval Bishop of Bath.

Document 4: Richard was a medieval Benedictine monk and Archbishop of Canterbury.
Much of Richard's time as archbishop was spent in a dispute with Roger de Pont L'Évêque.

Savaric spent many years attempting to annex Glastonbury Abbey as part of his bishopric.

Document 7: Æthelwig was an Abbot of Evesham before and during the Norman Conquest of England.
He was one of the few Englishmen trusted by the new King William the Conqueror.

The game received universal critical acclaim, including repeatedly being named 'Best PC Game of All Time'.
2: Fallout 3 is an action role-playing open world video game developed by Bethesda Game Studios.
Fallout 3 was critically acclaimed and received a number of Game of the Year awards.
3: The Atari video game burial was a mass burial of unsold video game cartridges, consoles, and computers in a New Mexico landfill site.
The event had become a cultural icon and a reminder of the North American video game crash of 1983.
4: Minecraft is a sandbox video game originally created by Swedish programmer Markus 'Notch' Persson.
Minecraft received five awards during the 2011 Game Developers Conference.
5: Super Smash Bros. is a series of crossover fighting games published by Nintendo.
The series achieved even greater success with the release of Super Smash Bros. Melee.
6: Insomniac Games is an American video game developer whose corporate headquarters is located in Burbank, California.
Insomniac Games has received recognition from critics as an acclaimed video game developer.
7: Age of Empires III is a real-time strategy video game developed by Microsoft Corporation's Ensemble Studios.
The game portrays the European colonization of the Americas.
8: Grand Theft Auto IV is an open world action-adventure video game developed by Rockstar North.
The game is played from a third-person perspective and its world is navigated on-foot or by vehicle.
9: The seventh generation includes consoles released since late 2005 by Nintendo, Microsoft, and Sony Computer Entertainment.
Video game consoles had become an important part of the global IT infrastructure.
10: The Operative: No One Lives Forever is a first-person shooter video game with stealth gameplay elements, developed by Monolith Productions.
The game was critically acclaimed for its stylistic representation of the era in the spirit of many spy films.

topic.name: American Revolutionary War Events
topic.description: Key military engagements and logistical operations during the American Revolutionary War.

1: Noble train of artillery , also known as the Knox Expedition , was an expedition led by Continental Army Colonel Henry Knox to transport heavy weaponry
Historian Victor Brooks has called Knox 's exploit 'one of the most stupendous feats of logistics' of the entire American Revolutionary War.
2: The Raid on Haverhill was a military engagement that took place on August 29 , 1708 during Queen Anne 's War.
French , Algonquin , and Abenaki warriors under the command of Jean-Baptiste Hertel de Rouville descended on Haverhill.
4: The Landing at Kip 's Bay was a British amphibious landing during the New York Campaign in the American Revolutionary War on September 15 , 1776.
Heavy advance fire from British naval forces in the East River caused the inexperienced militia guarding the landing area to flee.
5: The Battle of Hubbardton was an engagement in the Saratoga campaign of the American Revolutionary War fought in the village of Hubbardton , Vermont.
British forces , under General Simon Fraser , caught up with the American rear guard of the forces retreating after the withdrawal from Fort Ticonderoga.
6: The Battle of Kettle Creek was a major encounter in the back country of Georgia during the American Revolutionary War.
A militia force of Patriots decisively defeated and scattered a Loyalist militia force.
8: The Battle of Forts Clinton and Montgomery was an American Revolutionary War battle fought in the highlands of the Hudson River valley.
British forces under the command of General Sir Henry Clinton captured Fort Clinton and Fort Montgomery.
9: The Siege of Fort St. Jean was conducted by American Brigadier General Richard Montgomery on the town and fort of Saint-Jean in the British province of Quebec during the American Revolutionary War.
After several false starts in early September , the Continental Army established a siege around Fort St. Jean.
10: The Battles of Lexington and Concord were the first military engagements of the American Revolutionary War.
The battles marked the outbreak of open armed conflict between the Kingdom of Great Britain and thirteen of its colonies.

Some countries use the term medium-speed rail, or semi-high speed rail instead.
Document 2: STS-8 was the eighth NASA Space Shuttle mission and the third flight of the Space Shuttle Challenger.
The mission successfully achieved all of its planned research objectives.

Toyota manufactured the first generation Aurion between 2006 and 2012.

It was intended to be the first in a series of increasingly ambitious electric vehicles.

topic.name: Military History of New Zealand and Australia
topic.description: This topic encompasses the involvement and contributions of New Zealand and Australian military units during the World Wars, highlighting their formations, campaigns, and legacies.

Document 2: The Wellington Mounted Rifles Regiment was a mounted infantry regiment from New Zealand, raised for service during the First World War.
The regiment fought predominantly against the forces of the Ottoman Turkish Empire.
